description: A sandbox bypass vulnerability caused by undefined behavior in ShouldResetInterruptBudgetByICChange (tiering-manager.cc) when processing an out-of-range CachedTieringDecision enum value. The CachedTieringDecision enum has 6 valid variants (kPending=0 through kNormal=5), but the field is stored as a 3-bit value in the flags2 byte of SharedFunctionInfo objects, which live on the V8 sandbox heap and are therefore attacker-controllable. With 3 bits, values 0-7 are representable, meaning values 6 and 7 are invalid enum variants. When an attacker corrupts the cached_tiering_decision bits to an out-of-range value, the switch statement in ShouldResetInterruptBudgetByICChange has no matching case and no default branch, triggering undefined behavior. The compiler generates a ud2 (undefined instruction) for this unreachable path, causing an ILL crash that constitutes a V8 sandbox violation. The trigger path is: IC miss during property load -> LoadIC::UpdateCaches -> IC::OnFeedbackChanged -> TieringManager::NotifyICChanged -> ShouldResetInterruptBudgetByICChange with the corrupted enum.

fixes.evidence_text: 3517e6ced4eddbe54c1d468c91622494190984cc [sandbox] fix UB after switch on CachedTieringDecision enum
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6179823 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
